# Finalize · imprint review · v0.2.0

Source: `src/phases/finalize/imprint-review.ts` as of 2026-05-09.
Template variables use `{{var}}` here; actual builder uses `${...}` TS interpolation.

Changes from v0.1.0:
- Added explicit "OPERATOR IDENTITY" guidance block instructing T2 not to assume the reader IS the founder/president named in pillar_1 enrichment. Founder names are company facts (fine in description) but must NOT carry forward into operational instructions ("operator approval" not "Frank's approval").
- Added optional operatorGuidance parameter — free-form text the operator can inject via the new POST /wavex-os/onboarding/regenerate-imprint route to refine the imprint after initial finalize without re-running MC + connectors + swarm + workflows.
- Motivation: Ricoma onboarding produced an imprint that personalized to "Frank approves each gated task" because Pillar 1 enrichment surfaced President Frank Ma as a company fact. The operator using wavex-os may be the founder, an employee, an evaluator, or any third party — addressing the imprint to a specific person is an unsupported assumption.

---

You are generating an Imprint Review — a plain-language summary of an organization's complete Operator Ω manifest. Write 3–4 paragraphs, max 400 words total. Use the operator's own vocabulary. Be specific and concrete about company facts (products, prices, geography, MRR tier, agent counts). Do not use marketing language, do not say "I'm excited", do not use exclamation marks. Professional, plain, operational.

OPERATOR IDENTITY — IMPORTANT
The Pillar 1 enrichment may name founders, executives, or other people associated with the organization (e.g. "founded by Jane Doe", "President Bob Smith"). Those names are **company facts**, not the identity of the person reading this imprint. The operator using this onboarding may be the founder, an employee, a contractor, an investor doing diligence, or someone evaluating the company.

- DO NOT address the imprint to a specific person by name. NEVER write "Frank approves each gated task" or "Jane will need to review."
- DO refer to the reader generically: "the operator", "you", "whoever is operating this instance".
- DO mention founder/executive names when describing what the company is (e.g. "founded by Frank Ma" is fine in the company description), but DO NOT carry that name forward into operational instructions.
- The dry-run gates require **operator approval** (not "Frank's approval"). Phrase accordingly.{{guidance_block_optional}}

FULL CONTEXT
Organization: {{org_name}}
One-liner: {{company_context_truncated}}
Product state: {{product_state}} ({{stage}})
GTM profile: {{gtm_profile_enum}}
Board comms: {{comm_channel}}{{urgency_routing_suffix}}
Claude Code: {{claude_plan}} (verified)

Swarm: {{active_count}} of {{total_base_roster}} active, {{parked_count}} parked, {{disabled_count}} disabled.
Parked agents: {{parked_agents_csv}}
Disabled agents: {{disabled_agents_csv}}

Connectors required: {{connectors_required_csv}}
Connectors suggested: {{connectors_suggested_csv}}

Dry-run window: 14 days from {{dry_run_expires_at}}.
Dry-run gates (tasks held dry): {{dry_run_gates_count}} tasks across the swarm.

MC winner: {{mc_strategy_id}}
  sharpe: {{mc_sharpe}}
  mean MRR growth ({{mc_horizon_cycles}} cycles): {{mc_mean_mrr_growth_pct}}%
  p(auto-catalytic): {{mc_p_auto_catalytic_pct}}%
  p(ruin): {{mc_p_ruin_pct}}%
  cycles-to-critical: {{mc_cycles_to_critical}}

PARAGRAPH STRUCTURE
¶1 (who + current state): name the org, describe what it does, where it is on product + revenue, what the GTM motion looks like.
¶2 (deployed system): agent counts, notable parks/disables and why, connector stack, how Board gets notified.
¶3 (MC strategy): name the winning strategy in plain terms, expected cycles to criticality, the Sharpe read.
¶4 (dry-run expectations): 14 days, which tasks are gated, what the operator needs to approve before writes go live.

OUTPUT
Return ONLY the summary text. No JSON, no markdown headers, no salutations. Just 3–4 paragraphs.
